# `iterm2` sequences

<!-- Generated by `qdb generate reference` from `db/iterm2.toml`. Do not edit by hand. -->

## iTerm2 OSC 4 Default Background Query (`iterm2.osc4.default_background_query`)

iTerm2 extends OSC 4 reports with -2 for default background.

- Direction: host-to-terminal
- Syntax: `OSC 4 ; -2 ; ? ST`
- Responds with: `iterm2.osc4.default_background_report`
- References: [iTerm2 Proprietary Escape Codes](https://iterm2.com/documentation-escape-codes.html) (retrieved 2026-07-06)
- Fixtures: [`fixtures/iterm2/default_background_query.seq`](../../../fixtures/iterm2/default_background_query.seq)
- Conformance: alacritty no-reply; betamax no-reply; foot no-reply; kitty no-reply; tmux no-reply; wezterm no-reply; xterm no-reply

## iTerm2 OSC 4 Default Foreground Query (`iterm2.osc4.default_foreground_query`)

iTerm2 extends OSC 4 reports with -1 for default foreground.

- Direction: host-to-terminal
- Syntax: `OSC 4 ; -1 ; ? ST`
- Responds with: `iterm2.osc4.default_foreground_report`
- References: [iTerm2 Proprietary Escape Codes](https://iterm2.com/documentation-escape-codes.html) (retrieved 2026-07-06)
- Fixtures: [`fixtures/iterm2/default_foreground_query.seq`](../../../fixtures/iterm2/default_foreground_query.seq)
- Conformance: alacritty no-reply; betamax no-reply; foot no-reply; kitty no-reply; tmux no-reply; wezterm no-reply; xterm no-reply

## iTerm2 OSC 4 Default Background Report (`iterm2.osc4.default_background_report`)

Report form is source-inventoried but not decoded as a distinct event yet.

- Direction: terminal-to-host
- References: [iTerm2 Proprietary Escape Codes](https://iterm2.com/documentation-escape-codes.html) (retrieved 2026-07-06)
- Notes: reply syntax pending live capture

## iTerm2 OSC 4 Default Foreground Report (`iterm2.osc4.default_foreground_report`)

Report form is source-inventoried but not decoded as a distinct event yet.

- Direction: terminal-to-host
- References: [iTerm2 Proprietary Escape Codes](https://iterm2.com/documentation-escape-codes.html) (retrieved 2026-07-06)
- Notes: reply syntax pending live capture

## iTerm2 OSC 8 Anchor (`iterm2.osc8.anchor`)

Generic OSC 8 is typed as osc.8.hyperlink.

- Direction: host-to-terminal
- Syntax: `OSC 8 ; params ; url ST`
- References: [iTerm2 Proprietary Escape Codes](https://iterm2.com/documentation-escape-codes.html) (retrieved 2026-07-06)
- Fixtures: [`fixtures/iterm2/anchor.seq`](../../../fixtures/iterm2/anchor.seq)
- Notes: iTerm2 adds semantic-history handling for file URLs with line and column fragments.

## iTerm2 Cursor Shape (`iterm2.osc1337.cursor_shape`)

Values are block, vertical bar, and underline.

- Direction: host-to-terminal
- Syntax: `OSC 1337 ; CursorShape=N ST`
- References: [iTerm2 Proprietary Escape Codes](https://iterm2.com/documentation-escape-codes.html) (retrieved 2026-07-06)
- Fixtures: [`fixtures/iterm2/cursor_shape.seq`](../../../fixtures/iterm2/cursor_shape.seq)

## iTerm2 Steal Focus (`iterm2.osc1337.steal_focus`)

Requests that iTerm2 come to the foreground.

- Direction: host-to-terminal
- Syntax: `OSC 1337 ; StealFocus ST`
- References: [iTerm2 Proprietary Escape Codes](https://iterm2.com/documentation-escape-codes.html) (retrieved 2026-07-06)
- Fixtures: [`fixtures/iterm2/steal_focus.seq`](../../../fixtures/iterm2/steal_focus.seq)

## iTerm2 Clear Scrollback (`iterm2.osc1337.clear_scrollback`)

Erases scrollback history in supporting iTerm2 sessions.

- Direction: host-to-terminal
- Syntax: `OSC 1337 ; ClearScrollback ST`
- References: [iTerm2 Proprietary Escape Codes](https://iterm2.com/documentation-escape-codes.html) (retrieved 2026-07-06)
- Fixtures: [`fixtures/iterm2/clear_scrollback.seq`](../../../fixtures/iterm2/clear_scrollback.seq)

## iTerm2 Set Profile (`iterm2.osc1337.set_profile`)

Changes the current session profile.

- Direction: host-to-terminal
- Syntax: `OSC 1337 ; SetProfile=profile ST`
- References: [iTerm2 Proprietary Escape Codes](https://iterm2.com/documentation-escape-codes.html) (retrieved 2026-07-06)
- Fixtures: [`fixtures/iterm2/set_profile.seq`](../../../fixtures/iterm2/set_profile.seq)

## iTerm2 Notification (`iterm2.osc9.notification`)

Also covered by the generic OSC 9 notification helper.

- Direction: host-to-terminal
- Syntax: `OSC 9 ; message ST`
- References: [iTerm2 Proprietary Escape Codes](https://iterm2.com/documentation-escape-codes.html) (retrieved 2026-07-06)
- Fixtures: [`fixtures/iterm2/notification.seq`](../../../fixtures/iterm2/notification.seq)

## iTerm2 Progress Bar (`iterm2.osc9.progress`)

States include clear, progress, error, indeterminate, and warning.

- Direction: host-to-terminal
- Syntax: `OSC 9 ; 4 ; state ; percent ST`
- References: [iTerm2 Proprietary Escape Codes](https://iterm2.com/documentation-escape-codes.html) (retrieved 2026-07-06)
- Fixtures: [`fixtures/iterm2/progress.seq`](../../../fixtures/iterm2/progress.seq)

## iTerm2 Copy To Clipboard Mode (`iterm2.osc1337.copy_to_clipboard`)

Starts a mode where following received text is copied until EndCopy.

- Direction: host-to-terminal
- Syntax: `OSC 1337 ; CopyToClipboard=name ST`
- References: [iTerm2 Proprietary Escape Codes](https://iterm2.com/documentation-escape-codes.html) (retrieved 2026-07-06)
- Fixtures: [`fixtures/iterm2/copy_to_clipboard.seq`](../../../fixtures/iterm2/copy_to_clipboard.seq)

## iTerm2 End Copy Mode (`iterm2.osc1337.end_copy`)

Ends CopyToClipboard mode.

- Direction: host-to-terminal
- Syntax: `OSC 1337 ; EndCopy ST`
- References: [iTerm2 Proprietary Escape Codes](https://iterm2.com/documentation-escape-codes.html) (retrieved 2026-07-06)
- Fixtures: [`fixtures/iterm2/end_copy.seq`](../../../fixtures/iterm2/end_copy.seq)

## iTerm2 Tab Background Color (`iterm2.osc6.tab_background`)

Sets red, green, or blue tab chrome brightness.

- Direction: host-to-terminal
- Syntax: `OSC 6 ; 1 ; bg ; channel ; brightness ; N ST`
- References: [iTerm2 Proprietary Escape Codes](https://iterm2.com/documentation-escape-codes.html) (retrieved 2026-07-06)
- Fixtures: [`fixtures/iterm2/tab_background.seq`](../../../fixtures/iterm2/tab_background.seq)

## iTerm2 Tab Background Color Reset (`iterm2.osc6.tab_background_reset`)

Resets iTerm2 tab chrome background color.

- Direction: host-to-terminal
- Syntax: `OSC 6 ; 1 ; bg ; * ; default ST`
- References: [iTerm2 Proprietary Escape Codes](https://iterm2.com/documentation-escape-codes.html) (retrieved 2026-07-06)
- Fixtures: [`fixtures/iterm2/tab_background_reset.seq`](../../../fixtures/iterm2/tab_background_reset.seq)

## iTerm2 Set Colors (`iterm2.osc1337.set_colors`)

Supports named color keys, presets, tab default, and color-space prefixes.

- Direction: host-to-terminal
- Syntax: `OSC 1337 ; SetColors=key=value ST`
- References: [iTerm2 Proprietary Escape Codes](https://iterm2.com/documentation-escape-codes.html) (retrieved 2026-07-06)
- Fixtures: [`fixtures/iterm2/set_colors.seq`](../../../fixtures/iterm2/set_colors.seq)

## iTerm2 Deprecated OSC P Palette (`iterm2.osc_p.palette`)

Documented by iTerm2 as supported but not recommended.

- Direction: host-to-terminal
- Syntax: `OSC P n rr gg bb ST`
- References: [iTerm2 Proprietary Escape Codes](https://iterm2.com/documentation-escape-codes.html) (retrieved 2026-07-06)
- Fixtures: [`fixtures/iterm2/deprecated_palette.seq`](../../../fixtures/iterm2/deprecated_palette.seq)

## iTerm2 Add Annotation (`iterm2.osc1337.add_annotation`)

Supports message-only, length-delimited, and coordinate forms.

- Direction: host-to-terminal
- Syntax: `OSC 1337 ; AddAnnotation=... ST`
- References: [iTerm2 Proprietary Escape Codes](https://iterm2.com/documentation-escape-codes.html) (retrieved 2026-07-06)
- Fixtures: [`fixtures/iterm2/add_annotation.seq`](../../../fixtures/iterm2/add_annotation.seq)

## iTerm2 Add Hidden Annotation (`iterm2.osc1337.add_hidden_annotation`)

Same payload forms as AddAnnotation but does not immediately reveal the annotation.

- Direction: host-to-terminal
- Syntax: `OSC 1337 ; AddHiddenAnnotation=... ST`
- References: [iTerm2 Proprietary Escape Codes](https://iterm2.com/documentation-escape-codes.html) (retrieved 2026-07-06)
- Fixtures: [`fixtures/iterm2/add_hidden_annotation.seq`](../../../fixtures/iterm2/add_hidden_annotation.seq)

## iTerm2 Highlight Cursor Line (`iterm2.osc1337.highlight_cursor_line`)

Shows or hides the cursor guide.

- Direction: host-to-terminal
- Syntax: `OSC 1337 ; HighlightCursorLine=yes|no ST`
- References: [iTerm2 Proprietary Escape Codes](https://iterm2.com/documentation-escape-codes.html) (retrieved 2026-07-06)
- Fixtures: [`fixtures/iterm2/highlight_cursor_line.seq`](../../../fixtures/iterm2/highlight_cursor_line.seq)

## iTerm2 Request Attention (`iterm2.osc1337.request_attention`)

Values include yes, once, no, and fireworks.

- Direction: host-to-terminal
- Syntax: `OSC 1337 ; RequestAttention=value ST`
- References: [iTerm2 Proprietary Escape Codes](https://iterm2.com/documentation-escape-codes.html) (retrieved 2026-07-06)

## iTerm2 Background Image (`iterm2.osc1337.background_image`)

The value is a base64-encoded filename; iTerm2 requires user confirmation.

- Direction: host-to-terminal
- Syntax: `OSC 1337 ; SetBackgroundImageFile=base64 ST`
- References: [iTerm2 Proprietary Escape Codes](https://iterm2.com/documentation-escape-codes.html) (retrieved 2026-07-06)
- Fixtures: [`fixtures/iterm2/background_image.seq`](../../../fixtures/iterm2/background_image.seq)

## iTerm2 Report Cell Size Query (`iterm2.osc1337.report_cell_size_query`)

Requests cell height, width, and optional scale.

- Direction: host-to-terminal
- Syntax: `OSC 1337 ; ReportCellSize ST`
- Responds with: `iterm2.osc1337.report_cell_size_report`
- References: [iTerm2 Proprietary Escape Codes](https://iterm2.com/documentation-escape-codes.html) (retrieved 2026-07-06)
- Fixtures: [`fixtures/iterm2/report_cell_size_query.seq`](../../../fixtures/iterm2/report_cell_size_query.seq)
- Conformance: alacritty no-reply; betamax no-reply; foot no-reply; kitty no-reply; tmux no-reply; wezterm supported (31); xterm no-reply

## iTerm2 Report Cell Size Response (`iterm2.osc1337.report_cell_size_report`)

Scale is present in newer iTerm2 versions.

- Direction: terminal-to-host
- References: [iTerm2 Proprietary Escape Codes](https://iterm2.com/documentation-escape-codes.html) (retrieved 2026-07-06)

- Notes: reply syntax pending live capture

## iTerm2 Copy To Pasteboard (`iterm2.osc1337.copy_pasteboard`)

Writes a base64-encoded string to the system pasteboard.

- Direction: host-to-terminal
- Syntax: `OSC 1337 ; Copy=:base64 ST`
- References: [iTerm2 Proprietary Escape Codes](https://iterm2.com/documentation-escape-codes.html) (retrieved 2026-07-06)
- Fixtures: [`fixtures/iterm2/copy_pasteboard.seq`](../../../fixtures/iterm2/copy_pasteboard.seq)

## iTerm2 Report Variable Query (`iterm2.osc1337.report_variable_query`)

Requests an iTerm2 scripting variable by base64-encoded name.

- Direction: host-to-terminal
- Syntax: `OSC 1337 ; ReportVariable=base64 ST`
- Responds with: `iterm2.osc1337.report_variable_report`
- References: [iTerm2 Proprietary Escape Codes](https://iterm2.com/documentation-escape-codes.html) (retrieved 2026-07-06)

Returns the base64-encoded variable value.

- Direction: terminal-to-host
- References: [iTerm2 Proprietary Escape Codes](https://iterm2.com/documentation-escape-codes.html) (retrieved 2026-07-06)
- Notes: reply syntax pending live capture

## iTerm2 Set Badge Format (`iterm2.osc1337.set_badge_format`)

The badge format is an iTerm2 interpolated string encoded as base64.

- Direction: host-to-terminal
- Syntax: `OSC 1337 ; SetBadgeFormat=base64 ST`
- References: [iTerm2 Proprietary Escape Codes](https://iterm2.com/documentation-escape-codes.html) (retrieved 2026-07-06); [iTerm2 Badges](https://iterm2.com/documentation-badges.html) (retrieved 2026-07-06)
- Fixtures: [`fixtures/iterm2/set_badge_format.seq`](../../../fixtures/iterm2/set_badge_format.seq)

## iTerm2 Request Upload (`iterm2.osc1337.request_upload`)

Requests a user-selected tar.gz upload from iTerm2.

- Direction: host-to-terminal
- Syntax: `OSC 1337 ; RequestUpload=format=tgz ST`
- Responds with: `iterm2.osc1337.request_upload_response`
- References: [iTerm2 Proprietary Escape Codes](https://iterm2.com/documentation-escape-codes.html) (retrieved 2026-07-06)
- Fixtures: [`fixtures/iterm2/request_upload.seq`](../../../fixtures/iterm2/request_upload.seq)
- Conformance: alacritty unprobeable; betamax unprobeable; foot unprobeable; kitty unprobeable; tmux unprobeable; wezterm unprobeable; xterm unprobeable

## iTerm2 Request Upload Response (`iterm2.osc1337.request_upload_response`)

Response is not an OSC control string; it begins with ok or abort.

- Direction: terminal-to-host
- References: [iTerm2 Proprietary Escape Codes](https://iterm2.com/documentation-escape-codes.html) (retrieved 2026-07-06)
- Notes: reply syntax pending live capture

## iTerm2 Set Touch Bar Key Label (`iterm2.osc1337.set_key_label`)

Keys include F1 through F24 and status.

- Direction: host-to-terminal
- Syntax: `OSC 1337 ; SetKeyLabel=key=value ST`
- References: [iTerm2 Proprietary Escape Codes](https://iterm2.com/documentation-escape-codes.html) (retrieved 2026-07-06)
- Fixtures: [`fixtures/iterm2/set_key_label.seq`](../../../fixtures/iterm2/set_key_label.seq)

## iTerm2 Push Touch Bar Key Labels (`iterm2.osc1337.push_key_labels`)

Pushes current key labels onto a stack, optionally with a label.

- Direction: host-to-terminal
- Syntax: `OSC 1337 ; PushKeyLabels[=label] ST`
- References: [iTerm2 Proprietary Escape Codes](https://iterm2.com/documentation-escape-codes.html) (retrieved 2026-07-06)
- Fixtures: [`fixtures/iterm2/push_key_labels.seq`](../../../fixtures/iterm2/push_key_labels.seq)

## iTerm2 Pop Touch Bar Key Labels (`iterm2.osc1337.pop_key_labels`)

Pops key labels, optionally through the matching label.

- Direction: host-to-terminal
- Syntax: `OSC 1337 ; PopKeyLabels[=label] ST`
- References: [iTerm2 Proprietary Escape Codes](https://iterm2.com/documentation-escape-codes.html) (retrieved 2026-07-06)
- Fixtures: [`fixtures/iterm2/pop_key_labels.seq`](../../../fixtures/iterm2/pop_key_labels.seq)

## iTerm2 Unicode Width Version (`iterm2.osc1337.unicode_version`)

Controls iTerm2's Unicode width-table version and stack.

- Direction: host-to-terminal
- Syntax: `OSC 1337 ; UnicodeVersion=n|push[ label]|pop[ label] ST`
- References: [iTerm2 Proprietary Escape Codes](https://iterm2.com/documentation-escape-codes.html) (retrieved 2026-07-06)
- Fixtures: [`fixtures/iterm2/unicode_version.seq`](../../../fixtures/iterm2/unicode_version.seq)

## iTerm2 Multipart File Start (`iterm2.osc1337.multipart_file_start`)

Introduced for tmux integration mode and large file transfers.

- Direction: host-to-terminal
- Syntax: `OSC 1337 ; MultipartFile=args ST`
- References: [iTerm2 Proprietary Escape Codes](https://iterm2.com/documentation-escape-codes.html) (retrieved 2026-07-06); [iTerm2 Inline Images Protocol](https://iterm2.com/documentation-images.html) (retrieved 2026-07-06)
- Fixtures: [`fixtures/iterm2/multipart_file_start.seq`](../../../fixtures/iterm2/multipart_file_start.seq)

## iTerm2 Multipart File Part (`iterm2.osc1337.multipart_file_part`)

Carries one base64 payload chunk for a MultipartFile transfer.

- Direction: host-to-terminal
- Syntax: `OSC 1337 ; FilePart=base64 ST`
- References: [iTerm2 Proprietary Escape Codes](https://iterm2.com/documentation-escape-codes.html) (retrieved 2026-07-06); [iTerm2 Inline Images Protocol](https://iterm2.com/documentation-images.html) (retrieved 2026-07-06)
- Fixtures: [`fixtures/iterm2/multipart_file_part.seq`](../../../fixtures/iterm2/multipart_file_part.seq)

## iTerm2 Multipart File End (`iterm2.osc1337.multipart_file_end`)

Terminates a MultipartFile transfer.

- Direction: host-to-terminal
- Syntax: `OSC 1337 ; FileEnd ST`
- References: [iTerm2 Proprietary Escape Codes](https://iterm2.com/documentation-escape-codes.html) (retrieved 2026-07-06); [iTerm2 Inline Images Protocol](https://iterm2.com/documentation-images.html) (retrieved 2026-07-06)
- Fixtures: [`fixtures/iterm2/multipart_file_end.seq`](../../../fixtures/iterm2/multipart_file_end.seq)

## iTerm2 Custom Control Sequence (`iterm2.osc1337.custom`)

Used by iTerm2 scripts; includes a shared secret and script-defined pattern.

- Direction: host-to-terminal
- Syntax: `OSC 1337 ; Custom=id=secret:pattern ST`
- References: [iTerm2 Proprietary Escape Codes](https://iterm2.com/documentation-escape-codes.html) (retrieved 2026-07-06)
- Fixtures: [`fixtures/iterm2/custom.seq`](../../../fixtures/iterm2/custom.seq)

## iTerm2 Set User Variable (`iterm2.osc1337.set_user_var`)

Sets a user-defined variable used by iTerm2 substitutions.

- Direction: host-to-terminal
- Syntax: `OSC 1337 ; SetUserVar=name=base64 ST`
- References: [iTerm2 Proprietary Escape Codes](https://iterm2.com/documentation-escape-codes.html) (retrieved 2026-07-06)
- Fixtures: [`fixtures/iterm2/set_user_var.seq`](../../../fixtures/iterm2/set_user_var.seq)

## iTerm2 Shell Integration Version (`iterm2.osc1337.shell_integration_version`)

Reports the current version of the shell integration script.

- Direction: host-to-terminal
- Syntax: `OSC 1337 ; ShellIntegrationVersion=version[;shell] ST`
- References: [iTerm2 Proprietary Escape Codes](https://iterm2.com/documentation-escape-codes.html) (retrieved 2026-07-06)
- Fixtures: [`fixtures/iterm2/shell_integration_version.seq`](../../../fixtures/iterm2/shell_integration_version.seq)

## iTerm2 Remote Host (`iterm2.osc1337.remote_host`)

Reports user and fully qualified host name; OSC 7 is a related synonym.

- Direction: host-to-terminal
- Syntax: `OSC 1337 ; RemoteHost=user@host ST`
- References: [iTerm2 Proprietary Escape Codes](https://iterm2.com/documentation-escape-codes.html) (retrieved 2026-07-06)
- Fixtures: [`fixtures/iterm2/remote_host.seq`](../../../fixtures/iterm2/remote_host.seq)

## iTerm2 Clear Captured Output (`iterm2.osc1337.clear_captured_output`)

Erases iTerm2's current captured output.

- Direction: host-to-terminal
- Syntax: `OSC 1337 ; ClearCapturedOutput ST`
- References: [iTerm2 Proprietary Escape Codes](https://iterm2.com/documentation-escape-codes.html) (retrieved 2026-07-06)

## iTerm2 DECSCUSR 0 (`iterm2.csi.decscusr_zero`)

iTerm2 intentionally treats DECSCUSR 0 as reset to default cursor appearance.

- Direction: host-to-terminal
- Syntax: `CSI 0 SP q`
- References: [iTerm2 Proprietary Escape Codes](https://iterm2.com/documentation-escape-codes.html) (retrieved 2026-07-06)
- Fixtures: [`fixtures/iterm2/decscusr_zero.seq`](../../../fixtures/iterm2/decscusr_zero.seq)

## iTerm2 Curly Underline (`iterm2.csi.curly_underline`)

Also covered by generic colon-form SGR style handling.

- Direction: host-to-terminal
- Syntax: `CSI 4 : 3 m`
- References: [iTerm2 Proprietary Escape Codes](https://iterm2.com/documentation-escape-codes.html) (retrieved 2026-07-06)
- Fixtures: [`fixtures/iterm2/curly_underline.seq`](../../../fixtures/iterm2/curly_underline.seq)

## iTerm2 Extended Device Attributes (`iterm2.dcs.extended_device_attributes`)

Requests iTerm2 terminal name and version.

- Direction: host-to-terminal
- Syntax: `CSI > q`
- Responds with: `iterm2.dcs.extended_device_attributes_response`
- References: [iTerm2 Proprietary Escape Codes](https://iterm2.com/documentation-escape-codes.html) (retrieved 2026-07-06)
- Fixtures: [`fixtures/iterm2/extended_device_attributes_query.seq`](../../../fixtures/iterm2/extended_device_attributes_query.seq)
- Conformance: alacritty no-reply; betamax supported (16); foot supported (18); kitty supported (19); tmux supported (15); wezterm supported (38); xterm supported (16)

## iTerm2 Extended Device Attributes Response (`iterm2.dcs.extended_device_attributes_response`)

Reports terminal name and version, such as iTerm2 3.4.0.

- Direction: terminal-to-host
- References: [iTerm2 Proprietary Escape Codes](https://iterm2.com/documentation-escape-codes.html) (retrieved 2026-07-06)
- Fixtures: [`fixtures/iterm2/iterm2_dcs_extended_device_attributes_response_report_capture_tmux.seq`](../../../fixtures/iterm2/iterm2_dcs_extended_device_attributes_response_report_capture_tmux.seq), [`fixtures/iterm2/iterm2_dcs_extended_device_attributes_response_report_capture_betamax.seq`](../../../fixtures/iterm2/iterm2_dcs_extended_device_attributes_response_report_capture_betamax.seq), [`fixtures/iterm2/iterm2_dcs_extended_device_attributes_response_report_capture_kitty.seq`](../../../fixtures/iterm2/iterm2_dcs_extended_device_attributes_response_report_capture_kitty.seq), [`fixtures/iterm2/iterm2_dcs_extended_device_attributes_response_report_capture_wezterm.seq`](../../../fixtures/iterm2/iterm2_dcs_extended_device_attributes_response_report_capture_wezterm.seq), [`fixtures/iterm2/iterm2_dcs_extended_device_attributes_response_report_capture_foot.seq`](../../../fixtures/iterm2/iterm2_dcs_extended_device_attributes_response_report_capture_foot.seq), [`fixtures/iterm2/iterm2_dcs_extended_device_attributes_response_report_capture_xterm.seq`](../../../fixtures/iterm2/iterm2_dcs_extended_device_attributes_response_report_capture_xterm.seq)
- Notes: reply syntax pending live capture

## iTerm2 Open URL (`iterm2.osc1337.open_url`)

Requests opening a URL; iTerm2 requires permission and can disable it.

- Direction: host-to-terminal
- Syntax: `OSC 1337 ; OpenURL=:base64-url ST`
- References: [iTerm2 Proprietary Escape Codes](https://iterm2.com/documentation-escape-codes.html) (retrieved 2026-07-06)
- Fixtures: [`fixtures/iterm2/open_url.seq`](../../../fixtures/iterm2/open_url.seq)

## iTerm2 Disinter (`iterm2.osc1337.disinter`)

Requests disinterring a buried session.

- Direction: host-to-terminal
- Syntax: `OSC 1337 ; Disinter ST`
- References: [iTerm2 Proprietary Escape Codes](https://iterm2.com/documentation-escape-codes.html) (retrieved 2026-07-06)

## iTerm2 Block (`iterm2.osc1337.block`)

Defines the start or end of a code block.

- Direction: host-to-terminal
- Syntax: `OSC 1337 ; Block=id=identifier;attr=start|end ST`
- References: [iTerm2 Proprietary Escape Codes](https://iterm2.com/documentation-escape-codes.html) (retrieved 2026-07-06)
- Fixtures: [`fixtures/iterm2/block.seq`](../../../fixtures/iterm2/block.seq)

## iTerm2 Update Block (`iterm2.osc1337.update_block`)

Available in iTerm2 3.6.9 and later.

- Direction: host-to-terminal
- Syntax: `OSC 1337 ; UpdateBlock=id=identifier;action=fold|unfold ST`
- References: [iTerm2 Proprietary Escape Codes](https://iterm2.com/documentation-escape-codes.html) (retrieved 2026-07-06)
- Fixtures: [`fixtures/iterm2/update_block.seq`](../../../fixtures/iterm2/update_block.seq)

## iTerm2 Copy Button (`iterm2.osc1337.button_copy`)

Creates a copy button for a previously defined block.

- Direction: host-to-terminal
- Syntax: `OSC 1337 ; Button=type=copy;block=identifier ST`
- References: [iTerm2 Proprietary Escape Codes](https://iterm2.com/documentation-escape-codes.html) (retrieved 2026-07-06)
- Fixtures: [`fixtures/iterm2/button_copy.seq`](../../../fixtures/iterm2/button_copy.seq)

## iTerm2 Custom Button (`iterm2.osc1337.button_custom`)

Omitting code and icon invalidates existing custom buttons.

- Direction: host-to-terminal
- Syntax: `OSC 1337 ; Button=type=custom[;code=N;icon=symbol] ST`
- Responds with: `iterm2.csi.button_custom_event`
- References: [iTerm2 Proprietary Escape Codes](https://iterm2.com/documentation-escape-codes.html) (retrieved 2026-07-06)
- Fixtures: [`fixtures/iterm2/button_custom.seq`](../../../fixtures/iterm2/button_custom.seq)
- Conformance: alacritty unprobeable; betamax unprobeable; foot unprobeable; kitty unprobeable; tmux unprobeable; wezterm unprobeable; xterm unprobeable

## iTerm2 Custom Button Event (`iterm2.csi.button_custom_event`)

Sent by iTerm2 when a custom button is clicked.

- Direction: terminal-to-host
- References: [iTerm2 Proprietary Escape Codes](https://iterm2.com/documentation-escape-codes.html) (retrieved 2026-07-06)
- Notes: reply syntax pending live capture

## iTerm2 Feature Reporting (`iterm2.feature_reporting`)

Source-inventoried only: the escape-code page points feature detection to iTerm2's feature reporting protocol but does not define a stable OSC/CSI/DCS byte fixture here.

- Direction: bidirectional
- Syntax: `iTerm2 feature reporting protocol`
- References: [iTerm2 Proprietary Escape Codes](https://iterm2.com/documentation-escape-codes.html) (retrieved 2026-07-06)
- Notes: Keep this Known entry as an ownership reminder rather than a support claim until a source-backed query grammar and response model are added.
